agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tier-specific grace periods, and free trial abuse prevention.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, progressing through states: trial, active, renewing, grace period, cancelled."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect subscription fees. Has a validation status (valid/failed)."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring time period (e.g., monthly, annual) that defines when payment is collected."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The process of charging the payment method to continue the subscription into the next billing cycle."},{"term":"Renewal Attempt","definition":"A single try to collect payment during the renewal window. Up to 3 attempts before cancellation for standard tiers."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A 14-day window exclusive to Premium tier subscribers during which the subscription remains active despite failed payments, replacing the 3-strike cancellation rule."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A zero-cost subscription period offered to new customers, limited to one active trial per email address."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription level that receives enhanced billing leniency (14-day grace period instead of 3-strike rule)."},{"term":"Auto-Renewal Window","definition":"The 7-day period before a billing cycle ends during which the system initiates renewal attempts."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated when the payment method on file has a failed status.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription with a known-failed payment method would immediately create an inconsistent system state — an 'active' subscription that cannot collect revenue. This is not a policy threshold a VP could relax; it is a data consistency guard preventing the system from entering a fundamentally broken state where active subscriptions have no viable payment path.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Any call to activate a subscription WHERE paymentMethod.status == 'failed' MUST be rejected and the subscription MUST NOT transition to 'active'."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy decision balancing retry opportunity against premature charging. A product or finance leader could reasonably change this to 5 or 10 days without corrupting system state. The system would remain consistent with any positive lead time value.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: The renewal process for a subscription MUST be initiated exactly 7 days before the current billing cycle's end date."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-attempt threshold is a configurable business policy. Management could change it to 2 or 5 attempts without violating any data integrity constraint. The system state remains consistent regardless of the retry count — it is a tolerance knob, not a consistency guard.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a non-Premium subscription accumulates 3 consecutive failed renewal attempts, the system MUST transition the subscription status to 'cancelled' without manual intervention."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state machine integrity constraint. The 'cancelled' state is terminal by design. Allowing reactivation would break the subscription lifecycle invariant, create ambiguity around billing history, trial eligibility, and audit trails, and could mask fraud or payment abuse. This is not a policy preference — it enforces that the state graph has no backward edge from 'cancelled' to 'active'.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Any attempt to transition a subscription from 'cancelled' to 'active' MUST be rejected. The only valid path for the customer is creating a new Subscription aggregate."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 14-day duration and the decision to use a time-based grace period instead of attempt-based cancellation for Premium are business policy choices reflecting the higher value of Premium customers. A VP could change this to 21 days or revert Premium to the strike-based model. No system integrity is violated by adjusting this policy.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When a Premium tier subscription has a failed renewal, the subscription MUST remain active for 14 calendar days from the first failure before being cancelled, AND the 3-strike rule MUST NOT apply to Premium tier."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a uniqueness constraint protecting system integrity and preventing trial abuse. Allowing duplicate active trials per email would corrupt the business model by enabling unlimited free service. Like a unique key constraint in a database, this is a hard data consistency rule — not a preference. Relaxing it doesn't change a threshold; it removes a fundamental guard against exploitation.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When creating a free trial subscription, IF an active trial already exists for the same email address, the creation MUST be rejected. At no point may the system contain two Subscription records with status='trial' AND the same email address."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer attempts to activate a subscription","when":"The payment method on file has a 'failed' status","then":"The system rejects activation and returns an error indicating the payment method must be updated before activation"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription with a billing cycle ending in 7 days","when":"The auto-renewal scheduler runs","then":"The system initiates a renewal charge attempt against the stored payment method"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A non-Premium subscription with 2 prior failed renewal attempts","when":"The third renewal attempt also fails","then":"The system automatically transitions the subscription to 'cancelled' status and notifies the customer"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription in 'cancelled' status","when":"A customer or API call attempts to reactivate it","then":"The system rejects the reactivation and instructs the customer to create a new subscription"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Premium tier subscription with a failed renewal attempt","when":"The failure occurred fewer than 14 days ago","then":"The subscription remains active and the system continues retry attempts within the 14-day grace window"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A Premium tier subscription whose first renewal failure was 14 or more days ago","when":"Payment has still not succeeded","then":"The system automatically cancels the subscription"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An email address that already has an active free trial subscription","when":"A request is made to create another free trial for the same email","then":"The system rejects the request and returns an error indicating only one active trial per email is allowed"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Payment method fails between validation and activation","expected_behavior":"The activation must re-validate payment method status at the moment of state transition. A stale 'valid' check from minutes ago is insufficient — the system must enforce BR-1 at write time."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber's grace period expires on the same day as a successful retry payment","expected_behavior":"If payment succeeds before the cancellation job runs on day 14, the subscription remains active. The grace period check and payment success should be evaluated atomically or with payment success taking priority."},{"scenario":"Customer downgrades from Premium to Standard mid-grace-period","expected_behavior":"The system must decide whether to immediately apply the 3-strike rule or honor the remaining grace period. This is an ambiguity — see escalations."},{"scenario":"3rd renewal attempt fails but payment method is updated moments later","expected_behavior":"Cancellation triggered by the 3rd failure is final per BR-4. The customer must create a new subscription even if they fix payment seconds later."},{"scenario":"Customer uses email alias (user+tag@domain.com) to bypass trial uniqueness","expected_behavior":"The trial uniqueness check should normalize email addresses (strip aliases, case-normalize) to prevent trivial bypass of BR-6."},{"scenario":"Renewal window overlaps with a plan change or cancellation request","expected_behavior":"If a customer cancels during the 7-day renewal window but before the charge, the system should respect the cancellation and not initiate the renewal charge."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Correct enforcement of these rules directly protects revenue integrity. BR-1 prevents creating non-paying active subscriptions. BR-3/BR-5 balance revenue recovery (retries) against carrying bad debt. BR-6 prevents trial abuse that erodes conversion funnel economics.","operational":"Auto-renewal at 7 days provides the operations/support team a retry window to resolve payment issues before service disruption. The Premium grace period reduces churn for highest-value customers. Terminal cancellation simplifies state management and audit trails.","user":"Customers experience predictable billing behavior. Premium customers get leniency reflecting their value. The trial uniqueness rule may frustrate some users but protects the platform from abuse. The no-reactivation rule requires clear communication so customers understand they must re-subscribe.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) race conditions between payment retries and cancellation jobs, (2) email normalization gaps enabling trial abuse, (3) Premium-to-Standard downgrades during grace period creating ambiguous states. Compliance risk if cancellation/retry policies conflict with regional consumer protection laws.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (Standard tier)","impact":"Subject to 3-strike cancellation. Must maintain valid payment methods. Cannot reactivate cancelled subscriptions."},{"group":"Customers (Premium tier)","impact":"Receive 14-day grace period providing more time to resolve payment issues, reducing involuntary churn."},{"group":"Finance/Revenue team","impact":"Renewal timing and retry logic directly affect cash flow predictability and bad debt write-offs."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle queries about cancelled subscriptions that cannot be reactivated. Need clear scripts for directing customers to create new subscriptions."},{"group":"Engineering/Platform","impact":"Must implement robust state machine with terminal states, race condition handling, and email normalization."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is relatively well-defined with clear rules. Confidence is not higher because: (1) the interaction between Premium grace period and tier changes is unspecified, (2) email normalization strategy for trial uniqueness is not defined, (3) the relationship between the 7-day renewal window and the 3-attempt limit is ambiguous (are all 3 attempts within those 7 days, or spread across multiple cycles?).","high_confidence_areas":["BR-1: Payment validation gate on activation is unambiguous","BR-4: Terminal cancellation state is clearly stated","BR-6: One-trial-per-email uniqueness constraint is clear","Invariant classifications are well-grounded in the distinction between state integrity vs policy thresholds"],"low_confidence_areas":["Retry cadence within the 7-day renewal window is unspecified — are 3 attempts spread evenly across 7 days?","Premium grace period interaction with tier downgrades is undefined","Email normalization depth for trial uniqueness (aliases, dots, case) is not specified","Whether Premium grace period runs concurrently with or replaces retry attempts is unclear"],"assumptions":["The 3 renewal attempts for standard tier occur within the 7-day auto-renewal window","Premium tier grace period starts from the first failed renewal attempt, not from cycle end","Email uniqueness for trials is based on exact email match unless normalization is specified","Grace period and 3-strike are mutually exclusive paths — Premium never hits the 3-strike rule"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
